title: serde(other) Unknown variants silently lose original wire value on re-serialization
description: Six enums use #[serde(other)] Unknown: ChatKind, DeliveryState, ChatMemberRole, ChannelPermissionTargetType, ContactPresence, OwnerPresence. When one of these receives an unrecognized wire value and then serializes it back (e.g. in a JMAP set or a proxy path), the original string is lost and the literal string 'unknown' is emitted instead. This is documented in inline comments but is still a correctness hazard for any code path that reads and re-emits server state. BodyType and EndpointType already show the correct pattern: Unknown(String) with a manual serde impl that preserves the original string. Suggested rewrite: convert the six #[serde(other)] Unknown variants to Unknown(String) with manual Serialize/Deserialize impls (or a shared macro) matching the BodyType pattern. If any of these enums truly never need to round-trip unknown values, the doc comment should say so explicitly instead of warning that the value is lost.
notes: Investigation confirmed: ChatKind, ChatMemberRole, OwnerPresence all have outbound serialization paths. Filed as separate epic jmap-chat-tui-yw6v. ContactPresence is already guarded (contact.rs:87-91). DeliveryState, ChannelPermissionTargetType are safe (inbound only).
close_reason: Pre-existing issue; tracked in jmap-chat-tui-yw6v. Out of scope for enum-wiring epic.
